title: ci(cypress): add payment method blocking tests for business profile
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [x] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [x] CI/CD ## Description Add Cypress tests for Business Profile Payment Method Blocking functionality: - Card issuing country blocking - Card type blocking (debit/credit) - Card subtype blocking - Card network blocking ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context Test cases Enhancement ## How did you test it? Card supported connectors - stripe Card unsupported connectors - loonio ## Checklist - [ ] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [ ] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy` - [ ] I reviewed the submitted code - [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: likhinbopanna

reviews.body: BlockCardNetwork and BlockIssuers are defined in Commons.js but have no corresponding test cases in the spec file.

commits.message:
test(cypress): add payment method blocking tests for business profile

Add Cypress tests for Business Profile Payment Method Blocking functionality:
- Card issuing country blocking
- Card type blocking (debit/credit)
- Card subtype blocking
- Card network blocking

Changes:
- New test file: 00042-CardPaymentBlocking.cy.js
- Add blocked payment error body and test data to Commons.js
- Add blocklistToggle and UpdateBusinessProfileTest commands to commands.js
chore(cypress): run formatter and address lints

title: feat(core/connector): [SANTANDER] Add & Implement PushNotification & GenerateQr Connector Integration Flow
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description - Added a new flow type `PushNotification` & `GenerateQr` - The above two flows serves the purpose of a post-processing step wherein after the Authorize/SetupMandate Flow, either of `PushNotification` or `GenerateQr`is called. - The `PushNotification` flow makes a connector call to send a Push notification to the customer's banking app (requesting them to approve or reject the mandate) [Journey 1 of Pix Automatico] - The `GenerateQr` flow serves the purpose of making a GET call and receiving the QR Code Data from the connector [Journey 2/3/4 of Pix Automatico] - Integrated the `solicRec` api for `Journey 1 of Pix Automatico` in the `PushNotification` Flow in Santander - Integrated the `Consult & Activate Journey` api for `Journey 2/3/4 of Pix Automatico` in the `GenerateQr` Flow in Santander ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context ## How did you test it? For test cases refer to this [PR](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/pull/11589) ran `just clippy` & `just clippy_v2` ## Checklist - [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [x] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy` - [x] I reviewed the submitted code - [x] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: bsayak03

commits.message: feat(connector): add santander locrec session token flow
fix(connector): resolve santander locrec compile issues
feat(core): Adding new Payment Method Types : PixAutomaticoPush & PixAutomaticoQr
feat(core): Adding new Payment Method Types : PixAutomaticoPush & PixAutomaticoQr
fix: add authorize session token validation fn in ConnectorSpecifications trait
feat(core): Add CurrentFlowInfo in AccessToken Request Data and moved get_access_token_key to ConnectorSpecifications
fix: added comments for authorize session token connector specification trait fn
fix: got back the ConnectorAccessTokenSuffix trait
fix: removed connector logic for currentflowinfo in access token flow
feat(connector): [SANTANDER] Implement PaymentTrigger Flow for Pix Automatico (#11556)
fix: implemented payments trigger flow for santander
fix: implement payment trigger fn for Complete Authorize & Authorize Flow
fix: made connector changes in payments trigger flow
fix: payment trigger flow caller fixed
fix: refined some changes in payment trigger connector code
fix: removed unused try_from's and structs
fix(core): Renamed PaymentTrigger to PushNotification connector flow
feat(connector): [SANTANDER] Made connector changes for GenerateQr and PushNotification flows

title: feat(router): Add payment_session_id validation support to SdkAuthorization
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
Added `payment_session_id` validation in `SdkAuthorization`

A new `payment_session_id` is created in PaymentsCreate and its invalidated in `PaymentsUpdate`

Data stored in redis key: `payment_session:{merchant_id}:{payment_id}`

This PR validates that after a Payment Update, the merchant server informs the merchant client and the SDK of the update.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->
- In the current scenario, the merchant calls the Create Payment Intent ( POST /payments) API and gets the client secret.
- With this client secret, the merchant initiates the SDK. The SDK does /api/account/payment_methods, customers/payment_methods, and payments/session_tokens API calls and renders the SDK.
- If the merchant updates the payment intent with a S2S Update Intent API (POST /payments/{payment_id}) call, the SDK still works on the older context of the original payment intent.
- Since the SDK does not know the payment intent is updated, it does not re-render and confirms the payment with the old context of intent. This leads to a mismatch as the payment methods/ customer payment methods and sessions response can change with the updated intent.


Backend Implementation for Session ID

Requirements
- Ensure SDK authorization is extended to update intent and is validated in all the calls done by the client
- Create a session ID (UUID) construct that is returned in the sdk authorization
- Add validations on session ID - Only the latest session ID should be able to authenticate with the Backend.
- Since there are merchants using Update Intent flow, ensure maintenance of backward compatibility.

Steps
- Create a unique Session ID during payments create (get_trackers), attach it to payment_intent (persist the session ID in redis sdk_session:{payment_id} TTL = payment intent expiry) and send back the Session ID in SDKAuth in response
- Whenever there’s an Intent Update operation, refresh the session ID (get_trackers), invalidate the old ID (from Redis) and return the new ID in the SDK Auth
- When there’s any client call like Sessions, PML and Confirm if session ID is attached to SDK Auth it’s validated based on existence in redis
- If no session ID is present in the SDK auth, treat it as a legacy flow and skip session ID validation (Add merchant level metrics for no Session ID so that we can eventually make this count to 0 post migration)


## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

Summary for testing: If there is a payment update call, then the `sdk_authorization` string from the update response needs to be used for confirm (If using sdk_authorization for authentication). If the `sdk_authorization` from create response is used in this case, it will return an unauthorized error.

Payments Create

**Request:**

```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: dev_z5MBjdvfcXmflenVTSGTkKYCvLpmC9EVZyyIuJZUfAzetjznU1mSbBGhWQaMVX4T' \
--data-raw '{
    "amount": 6540,
    "currency": "USD",
    
    "confirm": false,
    

    
    "setup_future_usage":"on_session", 
    "customer": {
        "id": "acct_3f9GeQlxrowcmup1DfX9r",
        "name": "John Doe",
        "email": "customer@gmail.com",
        "phone": "9999999999",
        "phone_country_code": "+1"
    },
    
    

    "billing": {

        },

}'
```

title: feat(core/connector): [SANTANDER] Implement AuthorizeSessionToken (PayloadLocation) Flow for Pix Automatico
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
- Integrated the `Create Payload Location` (`/locrec`) API of Santander inside the `AuthorizeSessionToken` Connector Flow in Santander
- The location endpoint can be considered as a sessions call which is uniquely required in each payments lifecycle (CITs both 0$ and non zero $)

Reason for this addition of new flow : There was a step required after Authorize which calls the connectors and gets us the QR data 

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->


## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->
Refer to [PR](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/pull/11589) for the testcases

## Checklist
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy`
- [x] I reviewed the submitted code
- [x] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: bsayak03

title: feat(blocklist): introduce EligibilityPaymentMethodData for blocklist and eligibility checks
body:
## Type of Change

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description

Introduces a dedicated `EligibilityCard` and `EligibilityPaymentMethodData` type for use in blocklist and payment eligibility check flows.

Previously, the `PaymentsEligibilityRequest` and `should_payment_be_blocked` used the full `PaymentMethodData`/`Card` struct, which requires fields like CVV that are not relevant for eligibility checks. This PR adds a purpose-built type where only `card_number` is required.

### Additional Changes

- [x] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

## Motivation and Context

The eligibility check endpoint is used to determine whether a payment should be blocked (e.g. blocklist checks, profile-based card subtype blocking). Using the full `PaymentMethodData` type for this purpose was semantically incorrect — it implied all card fields (CVV, expiry, etc.) were required when they are not needed for blocklist lookups. A dedicated type makes the API contract clearer and reduces coupling between the eligibility flow and the full payment method data model.

## How did you test it?

<details>
<summary>1. eligiblity</summary>

Request
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments/pay_87FswLOPhRftIpE0ERqk/eligibility' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: [REDACTED]
--header 'Accept: */*' \
--data '{
    
    "payment_method_type": "card",
    "payment_method_subtype": "credit",
    "payment_method_data": {
        "card": {
            "card_number": "4000000000000000"
        }
    }
}
'
```

Response
```
{
    "error": {
        "error_type": "invalid_request",
        "message": "Json deserialize error: card number invalid at line 10 column 1",
        "code": "IR_06"
    }
}
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>2. eligiblity</summary>

Request
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments/pay_87FswLOPhRftIpE0ERqk/eligibility' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: [REDACTED]
--header 'Accept: */*' \
--data '{
    
    "payment_method_type": "card",
    "payment_method_subtype": "credit",
    "payment_method_data": {
        "card": {
            "card_number": "4000000000000002"
        }
    }
}
'
```

Response
```
{
    "payment_id": "pay_87FswLOPhRftIpE0ERqk",
    "sdk_next_action": {
        "next_action": {
            "deny": {
                "message": "Card number is blocklisted"
            }
        }
    }
}
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>3. eligiblity</summary>

Request
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments/pay_87FswLOPhRftIpE0ERqk/eligibility' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: [REDACTED]
--header 'Accept: */*' \
--data '
{
    "payment_method_type": "crypto",
    "payment_method_subtype": "crypto_currency",
    "payment_method_data": {
        "crypto": {
            "pay_currency": "ETH"
        }
    }
}
'
```

Response
```
{
    "payment_id": "pay_87FswLOPhRftIpE0ERqk",
    "sdk_next_action": {
        "next_action": "confirm"
    }
}
```
</details>

## Checklist

- [ ] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [ ] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy`
- [ ] I reviewed the submitted code
- [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: ayush22667

Adds a dedicated EligibilityCard and EligibilityPaymentMethodData type
where only card_number is required (no CVV), replacing the full
PaymentMethodData in eligibility and blocklist check flows.

fix(enums): correct serialization string for GeneralPurposeReLoadableCard
fix(enums): update serialization strings for GoldPlatinum and GoldPrem card subtypes
fix(enums): update serialization strings for GoldPlatinum and GoldPrem card subtypes

Merge branch 'feat/eligibility-payment-method-data-blocklist' of https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch into feat/eligibility-payment-method-data-blocklist

#	crates/router/src/core/blocklist/utils.rs

title: feat(core): Add `CurrentFlowInfo` in `AccessTokenRequestData` and moved `get_access_token_key` to `ConnectorSpecifications`
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->

In Journey 3/4 of Pix Automatico in Santander, we are calling `payments_operation_core` twice. The first time with `authorize_flow.rs` [Leg 1] and the second time with `setup_mandate_flow.rs`[Leg 2]. This is because the access token required in Leg 1 is different from the one required in Leg 2. This is because both the access token endpoints are different even though the api credentials are different. 

Hence,`AccessTokenAuth` connector flow should know when Leg 1 is being executed and when Leg 2 is being executed so that it can route them to different AccessToken endpoints. Therefore, we had to add `CurrentFlowInfo` in `AccessTokenRequestData`. Also the access token key should be different for Leg 1 and Leg 2, hence a match case based on `CurrentFlowInfo` was also added in the `get_access_token_key` fn inside the `ConnectorSpecifications` trait implementation. 

Also refractored the `get_access_token_key` from the `ConnectorAccessTokenSuffix` to `ConnectorSpecifications` since the trait fn's of `ConnectorSpecifications` already had `CurrentFlowInfo` being passed to its fn parameters.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

author: AkshayaFoiger

title: fix(paysafe): pass MCA metadata account_id to UCS connector config
body:
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->

Fix Paysafe UCS payment_method_tokenize failure where tokenize fails with "Failed to parse X-Connector-Config JSON into ConnectorSpecificConfig".

**Root cause:** `build_connector_config_header()` hardcoded `account_id: None` for Paysafe, never parsing the MCA metadata. The metadata has a nested structure `{"account_id": {"card": {"USD": {"no_three_ds": "..."}}}}` that needs to be extracted and passed to UCS.

**Additional constraint:** UCS proto-generated `PaysafePaymentMethodDetails` uses `HashMap` for `card`/`ach` fields (not `Option<HashMap>`). Proto map fields must always be present in JSON — absent fields cause deserialization failure (`missing field 'ach'`).

### Changes

- Added `PaysafeMetadata` wrapper struct to extract `account_id` from MCA metadata
- Changed `ConnectorSpecificConfig::Paysafe.account_id` from `Option<String>` → `Option<PaysafePaymentMethodDetails>`
- Added `#[serde(skip_serializing_if)]` on enum variant field to omit `account_id` when `None`
- Used `HashMap` (not `Option<HashMap>`) with `#[serde(default)]` for `card`/`ach` to match UCS proto expectations
- Added `PaysafeCardAccountId`, `PaysafeAchAccountId` structs with `skip_serializing_if` for inner Option fields

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->

## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->

The Paysafe UCS flow was failing during payment_method_tokenize with error "Failed to parse X-Connector-Config JSON into ConnectorSpecificConfig". This was blocking Paysafe UCS integration. The UCS connector expected account_id configuration from MCA metadata but Hyperswitch was not passing it.

## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

commits.message:
fix: extract Paysafe account_id from MCA metadata in UCS connector config

- Replace hardcoded account_id: None with proper extraction from metadata
- Navigate: metadata -> account_id -> card -> USD -> no_three_ds -> string value
- Add error handling for missing metadata
- Wrap result in Some() for account_id field

Resolves 'Invalid Configuration: account_id' error in payment_method_tokenize gRPC call
fix(ucs): [paysafe] fix invalid account id in ucs due to proto bump

title: refactor(router): handle dispute webhook validation failure with 4xx response
body: ## Type of Change - [x] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [x] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description When an unexpected dispute status was encountered, we previously returned a WebhookProcessingFailure error, which resulted in a 5xx response. With this PR, this behavior has been changed to return a 4xx response instead in such scenarios. Hotfix for https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/pull/11607 ## How did you test it? Locally tested by manually triggering dispute webhook Create a payment ``` curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'Accept: title: feat(ucs): Bump UCS client dependency to bring latest changes
body:
## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [x] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description Bumped UCS dependency to bring latest changes ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context ## How did you test it? Tested Through Cypress Tests:- **Adyen:-** 1. Authorize:- 2. Capture:- 3. Void:- 4. Refund:- 5. Forced PSync:- No diff found **Paypal:-** 1. Authorize:- 2. PSync:- 3. Capture:- 4. Void:- 5. title: feat(connectors): [Revolv3] Added Debit as a supported payment method in feature matrix
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [x] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
- Updated `REVOLV3_SUPPORTED_PAYMENT_METHODS` in `revolv3.rs` to include Debit card payments
- Debit cards have the same feature support as Credit cards:
  - Mandates: Supported
  - Refunds: Supported
  - Capture methods: Automatic, Manual, SequentialAutomatic
  - Supported card networks: Visa, Mastercard, AmericanExpress, Discover, JCB, DinersClub, UnionPay
  - 3DS: Not Supported, No 3DS: Supported



## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

title: feat(core): Introduce New Operation to Accommodate Santander CIT Flow
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->

### Context

The Santander CIT flow involves the following sequence of API calls:

1. Create Access Token
2. Create Charge (Authorize)
3. Create Access Token
4. Create Location
5. Create Recurrence (Setup Mandate)
6. Get QR Data

These steps are logically divided into two legs:

**First Leg: Steps 1–2**
The first access token is used to create the charge (authorize).

**Second Leg: Steps 3–6**
A new access token is generated and used for location creation, recurrence (mandate setup), and fetching QR data.

### Implementation Details

To support this flow, we invoke `payments_operation_core` twice:

**First Invocation**
**Flow**: Authorize
Covers the first leg (Access Token + Charge creation)

**Second Invocation**
**Flow**: SetupMandate
Covers the second leg (Access Token + Location + Recurrence + QR Data)

### New Operation: PaymentRecurrence
Introduced a new **operation** `PaymentRecurrence` to handle the second leg.
This operation behaves similarly to the PaymentsConfirm flow: retrieves `payment_data` via `get_trackers`.

### Validation Logic
`PaymentRecurrence` enforces that the payment status must be `requires_customer_action` to proceed.
This aligns with the current behavior where, after charge creation (Santander step 2), the payment status is explicitly set to `requires_customer_action`.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

1. `config`

9. `loadtest/config`
-->

-->

title: fix(connectors): [paypal] handle optional vault fields in 3DS CIT responses
body: ## Description PayPal returns APPROVED status (without vault.id and customer) for async vaulting and VAULTED status (with vault.id and customer) for synchronous vaulting. This fix makes vault.id and customer optional to handle both scenarios: - APPROVED: Payment succeeds, no mandate stored (vault.id arrives later via webhook) - VAULTED: Payment succeeds, mandate stored with vault.id Changes: - Made PaypalVaultResponse.id and customer optional (Option) Before this, id and customer were mandatory fields, but were missing from the complete authorize call when the vault status is APPROVED, causing deserialization errors and 5xx responses for few CIT transaction in PROD. Reference: https://developer.paypal.com/docs/checkout/save-payment-methods/during-purchase/js-sdk/cards/#link-saveapprovedpaymentsource ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## How did you test it? author: swangi-kumari

commits.message:
fix(connectors): [paypal] handle optional vault fields in 3DS CIT responses

PayPal returns APPROVED status (without vault.id and customer) for async vaulting
and VAULTED status (with vault.id and customer) for synchronous vaulting.

This fix makes vault.id and customer optional to handle both scenarios:
- APPROVED: Payment succeeds, no mandate stored (vault.id arrives later via webhook)
- VAULTED: Payment succeeds, mandate stored with vault.id

Changes:
- Made PaypalVaultResponse.id and customer optional (Option<T>)
- Updated mandate reference extraction to use and_then for Option handling

Reference: https://developer.paypal.com/docs/checkout/save-payment-methods/during-purchase/js-sdk/cards/#link-saveapprovedpaymentsource

title: feat(payments): extend MIT via card + NTI flows for modular merchants
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [x] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->

This PR adds the Card+NTI MIT behavior in for modular merchants.

### What changed
- Added `NetworkMandateId` handling in `make_modular_pm_data`:
  - `CardWithOptionalCVC` now converts to `PaymentMethodData::CardDetailsForNetworkTransactionId` for network-mandate-selected flows.
- Preserved existing behavior:
  - `ConnectorMandateId` still maps to `MandatePayment`.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->
This change ensures Card+NTI-selected flows are converted into `CardDetailsForNetworkTransactionId` and proceed correctly, while keeping legacy and existing modular PSP-token behavior untouched.

## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

title: feat(pm-modular): move behind feature flags
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [x] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->

This PR adds feature flags for PM modular fork points in v1 payments core by introducing a new `router` feature flag: `pm_modular`.

Default build path (`not(feature = "pm_modular")`) now compiles the legacy flow in:
- `payments_core`
- `get_trackers` interfaces/call paths
- post/update-tracker mandate + PM persistence path

Forked PM modular flow is preserved behind `#[cfg(feature = "pm_modular")]` so it can be restored quickly via feature selection.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [x] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->

This change removes PM modular forking in v1 payments core.
The goal is to keep the default binary fork-free and preserve existing modular behavior behind a compile-time flag (`pm_modular`) for quick enablement whenever needed.

## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

- Built/checked router in both feature variants:
  - `cargo check -p router` (default non-`pm_modular` path)
  - `cargo check -p router --features pm_modular` (forked modular path)
- Ran clippy with `pm_modular` included in feature set to validate cfg-gated compilation/lints on modular path.

## Checklist
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy`
- [x] I reviewed the submitted code
- [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: kashif-m

title: feat(authentication): Generate authn values in response for pci merchant using modular auth
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
Added Authentcation details to post_authentication api response for PCI merchants to use.

### Additional Changes

- [x] This PR modifies the API contract

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->

have added `should_disable_vault_tokenization_{merchant_id}` for tacking PCI merchants to enable or disable populating PCI values

## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->

Currently when a PCI merchant is using modular authentication it is not able to get the authentication details since we had mandated vaulting! Now if the merchant is PCI compliant should be able to get the value and carry forward Authorization on its own if required.

## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

Create JuspayThreedsServer MCA

```shell
curl --location 'localhost:8080/account/sahkal11111/connectors' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: dev_UZm3FY2GrNS4Ox04rvFXOx7eK6TSW6qgyxdbbwz78khSigSLrSIayZh5seZadK52' \
--data '
{
    "connector_type": "authentication_processor",
    "connector_name": "juspaythreedsserver",
    "connector_account_details": {
        "auth_type": "NoKey"
    },
    "test_mode": true,
    "disabled": false,
            "metadata": {
        "merchant_category_code": "5411",
        "merchant_country_code": "840",
        "merchant_name": "Dummy Merchant",
        "pull_mechanism_for_external_3ds_enabled": true
    }
}
'
```

{

title: feat(blocklist): introduce EligibilityPaymentMethodData for blocklist and eligibility checks
body: ## Type of Change - [x] New feature - [x] Enhancement ## Description Introduces `EligibilityPaymentMethodData` as a dedicated type for blocklist and eligibility checks, decoupling it from the broader `PaymentMethodData` type. Adds conversions from `CardWithOptionalCVC` and `CardWithNetworkTokenDetails` into `EligibilityCard`, and fixes enum serialization strings for `GoldPlatinum`, `GoldPrem`, and `GeneralPurposeReLoadableCard` card subtypes. ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context Hotfix cherry-pick of #11654 into `hotfix-2026.04.01.0`. Closes #11697 ## How did you test it? Verified cherry-picks apply cleanly and compile correctly against the hotfix base. ## Checklist - [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [ ] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy` - [ ] I reviewed the submitted code - [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: ayush22667

commits.message:
feat(blocklist): introduce EligibilityPaymentMethodData for blocklist and eligibility checks

Adds a dedicated EligibilityCard and EligibilityPaymentMethodData type
where only card_number is required (no CVV), replacing the full
PaymentMethodData in eligibility and blocklist check flows.
feat(eligibility): implement conversions for EligibilityCard from CardWithOptionalCVC and CardWithNetworkTokenDetails
fix(enums): correct serialization string for GeneralPurposeReLoadableCard
fix(enums): update serialization strings for GoldPlatinum and GoldPrem card subtypes
fix(enums): update serialization strings for GoldPlatinum and GoldPrem card subtypes

title: feat(superposition): add superposition seeding script for default configs & dimensions
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
This PR adds support for seeding superposition default configs and dimensions with just  & docker. 
```
# Full Docker setup (auto-seeded)
docker-compose up -d

# Or with just commands
just superposition-up
just superposition-seed  # if needed manually
cargo run
```

Why do we need to seed superposition dimensions and default configs ?

Schema for default configs needs a tracking and standardized for Hyperswitch. superposition_seed.json file will help us in tracking and standardizing schema changes in superposition. 

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema

```

title: feat(payouts): add payout sync workflow to process tracker
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
This add the following changes to enable polling with payout retrieve:
- Add payout sync workflow to consumer
- Enable payout sync workflow in producer
- Add method to trigger the workflow in payouts core
- Add config to customize frequencies, count and max_retry_count.

Following Superposition changes are added as well:
- Create a new Dimension (connector)
- Add new key for payout sync tracker

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

Tested through Postman:

Create an MCA (Paypal Payout):

Add config for payout sync process tracker (if not set, it will take default values):
```
curl --location '{{baseUrl}}/configs/' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'api-key:{{admin-api-key}}' \
--data '{
    "key": "payout_tracker_mapping_paypal",
    "value": "{\"custom_merchant_mapping\":{},\"default_mapping\":{\"start_after\":10,\"frequencies\":[[5,5],[2,2]]},\"max_retries_count\":10}"
}'
```
Create an Payout via Paypal:
```
{
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "GBP",
    "customer_id": "payout_customer",
    "email": "payout_customer@example.com",
    "name": "John Doe",
    "phone": "999999999",
    "phone_country_code": "+65",
    "description": "Its my first payout request",
    "payout_type": "wallet",
    "payout_method_data": {
        "wallet": {
            "paypal": {
                "telephone_number": "16608213349"
            }
        }
    },
    "billing": {
        "address": {
            "line1": "1467",
            "line2": "Harrison Street",
            "line3": "Harrison Street",
            "city": "San Fransico",
            "state": "NY",
            "zip": "94122",
            "country": "US",
            "first_name": "John",
            "last_name": "Doe"
        },
        "phone": {
            "number": "8056594427",
            "country_code": "+91"
        }
    },
    "entity_type": "NaturalPerson",
    "recurring": false,
    "metadata": {
        "ref": "123"
    },
    "confirm": true,
    "auto_fulfill": true
}
```
Response should be `pending`
```

title: feat(router): added superposition api support to fetch sdk configs
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->
- [x] New feature

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
added superposition api support to fetch sdk configs

## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->

Added new api endpoint to fetch `raw_configs` and `resolved_configs` from superposition.
We will be using superposition client to fetch all the dynamic fields based on merchant requirements

```shell
domain_name/v1/sdk/configs/{profile_id}/{platform}/{sdk_config.json}
```

## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->
Request
```shell
curl --location 'localhost:8003/v1/sdk/configs/pro_wkuehdgiuwhd/web/sandbox.json' \
--header 'api-key: pk_dev_53d53c92081d4a7a8199b5283d3d07b8' \
--data ''
```

Response

<details>
 <summary>superposition response</summary>

title: feat(payments): extend MIT use cases for modular merchants
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
This PR extends MIT support for modular merchants. Includes below MIT flows

- Off Session MITs (S2S)
- On Session MITs (confirmed by client)

MIT flow is added only for PSP tokens.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context
This PR helps in extending the MIT support for modular merchants.

## How did you test it?

## Test Case: Repeat CIT for Modular Merchants

### Pre-requisites

<details>
    <summary>Create a customer</summary>

cURL

        --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \

        --header 'x-profile-id: pro_UdaqXHD6kUf04kWzovns' \
        --data-raw '{

            "email": "albert@gmail.com",

title: feat(platform): add cypress tests for platform
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description This pull request adds comprehensive end-to-end Cypress tests for platform merchant flows, including merchant onboarding, profile setup, connector configuration, and customer management. The tests cover multiple merchant types (platform, connected, standard), validate access control, and ensure correct state management across scenarios. The changes are grouped by functional area and provide thorough coverage of platform behaviors. Merchant onboarding and API key management: * Added tests in `00001-PlatformSetup.cy.js` for creating platform, connected, and standard merchants, assigning API keys, and verifying merchant listing within organizations. Profile creation and access control: * Added tests in `00002-ProfileSetup.cy.js` to verify profile creation rules for different merchant types, including restrictions for platform merchants and proper profile assignment for connected and standard merchants. Connector setup and validation: * Added tests in `00003-ConnectorSetup.cy.js` for creating and retrieving connectors for connected and standard merchants, verifying connector separation, and enforcing connector creation restrictions for platform and standard merchants. Customer flows and access control: * Added tests in `00004-CustomerFlows.cy.js` to validate customer sharing across connected merchants, ensure proper access restrictions for standard merchants, and confirm customer visibility and isolation as per merchant type. ## How did you test it? ## Checklist - [ ] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [ ] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy` - [ ] I reviewed the submitted code - [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: AnkitKmrGupta

title: fix(users): update permission to create custom roles
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [x] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description

Updated custom role creation permission checks so profile-level users with `UsersManage` can create custom roles for the profile entity type.

- Updated permission guard for:
  - `create_role`
  - `create_role_v2`
- Changed from `Permission::MerchantUserWrite` to `Permission::ProfileUserWrite` 

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context

Currently, profile-level users are blocked from creating custom roles due to restrictive permission checks in both backend and frontend. Since merchant-level users can already create custom roles for both merchant and profile entity types, profile users with `UsersManage` should also be allowed to create roles for the profile entity type.

This change removes that inconsistency and enables expected profile-level role management.


## How did you test it?

- Verified backend route-level permission updates for custom role creation endpoints.
- Manually validated the custom role creation flow for profile-level users with `UsersManage`.
- Confirmed that profile-level users can create profile-entity custom roles after the change.
- Should be only able to create with profile scope and profile entity_type

Request
```bash
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/user/role/v2' \
--header 'authorization: Bearer *****' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{
    "role_scope": "profile",
    "role_name": "role_123",
    "entity_type": "profile",
    "parent_groups": [
        {
            "name": "Connectors",
            "scopes": [
                "write",
                "read"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "Workflows",
            "scopes": [
                "write",
                "read"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "Account",
            "scopes": [
                "write",
                "read"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "Operations",
            "scopes": [
                "write",
                "read"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "Users",
            "scopes": [
                "write",
                "read"
            ]
        }
    ]
}'
```

Response:
200 

Request
```bash
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/user/user/update_role' \
--header 'authorization: Bearer *****' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{"email":"xyz+234@juspay.in","role_id":"profile_admin"}'
```

Response:
200 

## Checklist
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy`
- [x] I reviewed the submitted code
- [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible

title: feat(payments): extend MIT via network token + NTI flows for modular merchants
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
This PR adds support for modular NT + NTI flows in payments service.

- Added a new domain PMD variant:
  - `CardWithNetworkTokenDetails(CardWithNetworkTokenDetails)`
  - includes both card details (optional CVC) and network token details.
- Extended modular raw PM API to support combined card+NT payload:
  - `RawPaymentMethodData::CardWithNT`.
- In modular retrieve flow:
  - [Modular Service] fetch raw card + raw network-token data from vault,
  - [Modular Service] merge into `RawPaymentMethodData::CardWithNT`,
  - [Payments Service] transform into `PaymentMethodData::CardWithNetworkTokenDetails`.
- Extended `make_modular_pm_data` conversion logic:
  - `NetworkTokenWithNTI` -> `PaymentMethodData::NetworkToken`
  - fallback -> `Card` (with CVC validation preserved)
- Updated NT+NTI action selection path to support modular aggregated PMD:
  - `get_all_action_types` / `is_network_token_with_network_transaction_id_flow` now accept modular PMD context and can build NT+NTI actions from modular data without status-check dependency.

### Additional Changes

- [x] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->

## Motivation and Context
Allows modular merchants to execute NT+NTI flows.

## How did you test it?

title: fix(connector): [ACI] Map Successful Status of Cancel to Voided AttemptStatus
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->

In ACI, when a transaction is being successfully cancelled, the `IntentStatus` becomes `Succeeded` instead of `Cancelled`.
Additionally, the webhook flow in ACI is encountering `Internal Server Error` due to a miss in renaming the enum variant.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->


## How did you test it?
<!--
Did you write an integration/unit/API test to verify the code changes?
Or did you test this change manually (provide relevant screenshots)?
-->

title: feat(router): add card_discovery dimension to 3ds decision rule engine
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description

Add card_discovery as a new dimension in the 3DS decision rule engine to allow rules based on how the card was discovered during checkout:
- Manual: New card entry (user enters full PAN)
- SavedCard: Stored card token (COF)
- ClickToPay: Click to Pay

This enables rules like:
- If card_discovery = saved_card, then request a 3DS challenge
- If card_discovery = manual AND amount > 50 EUR, then mandate a 3DS challenge

Changes:
- Export CardDiscovery enum in euclid crate
- Add CardDiscovery to DirKeyKind and DirValue enums
- Add card_discovery field to PaymentMethodInput
- Add DirKeyKind::CardDiscovery to allowed 3DS dimensions
- Add card_discovery to API models
- Pass card_discovery from payment_attempt to 3DS decision rule


### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

<!--
Provide links to the files with corresponding changes.

Following are the paths where you can find config files:
1. `config`
2. `crates/router/src/configs`
3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->


## How did you test it?

Manual Testing

Create a CardDiscovery-based 3DS rule:
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/account/three_ds_decision_rule' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: test_admin' \
--header 'X-Profile-Id: {{profile_id}}' \
--header 'X-Merchant-Id: {{merchant_id}}' \
--data '{
    "name": "card_discovery_3ds_rule",
    "algorithm": {
        "type": "rule_based",
        "data": {
            "rules": [
                {
                    "name": "manual_card_3ds",
                    "connectorSelection": {
                        "three_ds_authentication_type": "three_ds"
                    },
                    "statements": [
                        {
                            "condition": [
                                {
                                    "lhs": "card_discovery",
                                    "comparison": "equal",
                                    "value": {
                                        "type": "enum_variant",
                                        "value": "manual"
                                    },
                                    "metadata": {}
                                }
                            ]
                        }
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "name": "saved_card_no_3ds",
                    "connectorSelection": {
                        "three_ds_authentication_type": "no_three_ds"
                    },
                    "statements": [
                        {
                            "condition": [
                                {
                                    "lhs": "card_discovery",
                                    "comparison": "equal",
                                    "value": {
                                        "type": "enum_variant",
                                        "value": "saved_card"
                                    },
                                    "metadata": {}
                                }
                            ]
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ],
            "defaultSelection": {
                "three_ds_authentication_type": "no_three_ds"
            }
        }
    }
}'
```

Activate the rule (use the rule_id from the response above):
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/account/three_ds_decision_rule/{{rule_id}}/activate' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: test_admin' \
--header 'X-Profile-Id: {{profile_id}}' \
--header 'X-Merchant-Id: {{merchant_id}}'
```
---

Test Cases

Test 1: Manual card entry -> expects three_ds (matches card_discovery = manual rule)
Create payment
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: {{api_key}}' \
--data '{
    "amount": 2540,
    "currency": "USD",
    "confirm": false,
    "customer_id": "cus_test_001",
    "profile_id": "{{profile_id}}",
    "payment_method": "card",
    "payment_method_type": "credit"
}'
```
Confirm payment (manual card — no token)
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments/{{payment_id}}/confirm' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: {{api_key}}' \
--data '{
    "payment_method": "card",
    "payment_method_type": "credit",
    "payment_method_data": {
        "card": {
            "card_number": "4111111111111111",
            "card_exp_month": "03",
            "card_exp_year": "2030",
            "card_holder_name": "Test User",
            "card_cvc": "737"
        }
    }
}'
```
> Expected: authentication_type: "three_ds" — card_discovery is manual (fresh card number, no token)
---
Test 2: Saved card -> expects no_three_ds (matches card_discovery = saved_card rule)
Create payment with mandate customer acceptance for saved card flow
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: {{api_key}}' \
--data '{
    "amount": 2540,
    "currency": "USD",
    "confirm": false,
    "customer_id": "cus_test_001",
    "profile_id": "{{profile_id}}",
    "payment_method": "card",
    "payment_method_type": "credit",
    "setup_future_usage": "off_session",
    "customer_acceptance": {
        "acceptance_type": "online",
        "accepted_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
        "online": {
            "ip_address": "127.0.0.1",
            "user_agent": "test-agent"
        }
    }
}'
```
Confirm with saved card token (use payment_token from a previously saved card)
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/payments/{{payment_id}}/confirm' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'api-key: {{api_key}}' \
--data '{
    "payment_method": "card",
    "payment_method_type": "credit",
    "payment_token": "{{saved_payment_token}}",
    "card_cvc": "737"
}'
```
> Expected: authentication_type: "no_three_ds" — card_discovery is saved_card (using stored payment token)



## Checklist
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [ ] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy`
- [ ] I reviewed the submitted code
- [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: akash-c-k

commits.message:
feat(router): add card_discovery dimension to 3ds decision rule engine

Add card_discovery as a new dimension in the 3DS decision rule engine to allow
rules based on how the card was discovered during checkout:
- Manual: New card entry (user enters full PAN)
- SavedCard: Stored card token (COF)
- ClickToPay: Click to Pay

This enables rules like:
- If card_discovery = saved_card, then request a 3DS challenge
- If card_discovery = manual AND amount > 50 EUR, then mandate a 3DS challenge

Changes:
- Export CardDiscovery enum in euclid crate
- Add CardDiscovery to DirKeyKind and DirValue enums
- Add CardDiscovery to EuclidKey and EuclidValue types
- Add card_discovery field to PaymentMethodInput
- Add DirKeyKind::CardDiscovery to allowed 3DS dimensions
- Add card_discovery to API models
- Add CardDiscovery lowering in AST and DIR modules
- Pass card_discovery from payment_attempt to 3DS decision rule

fix(router): complete card_discovery wiring in 3ds decision rule engine

- Add card_discovery field to all PaymentMethodInput constructions in routing, benchmarks, and tests
- Insert card_discovery into euclid Context enum_values so the rule engine evaluates it
- Add CardDiscovery variant mapping in euclid_wasm get_variant_values
- Compute card_discovery before 3DS rule evaluation in payment_confirm to fix sequencing
- Fix borrow/move error for card_network in 3ds decision rule utils
- Remove pr_body.md that was accidentally committed
fix(router): remove unused CardDiscovery import in api_models
refactor(router): remove intermediate let binding for card_discovery assignment

title: feat(payouts): add v2 filter for profile
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
Currently filter, Current endpoint : payouts/profile/filter
adds v2 for payout profile filter with below behaviours:

the connectors can be filtered using label
the connectors can be filtered using merchant_connector_id
the connectors can be filtered using currencies

title: feat(platform): platform payment cypress tests
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description This pull request introduces comprehensive end-to-end Cypress test coverage for platform and connected merchant payment flows, including 3DS, no-3DS, and manual capture scenarios. It also enhances the global state handling to support these new test cases by storing additional keys required for platform and merchant contexts. **New E2E Payment Flow Tests:** * Added three new Cypress test files to cover different payment flows: - [`00006-NoThreeDSAutoCapture.cy.js`](diffhunk://#diff-e1393b2e4b3b130394efae31621155088dd26c189ca952cbd929bf6d9e382781R1-R186): Tests platform and connected merchant "No 3DS" auto-capture flows. - [`00007-ThreeDSAutoCapture.cy.js`](diffhunk://#diff-15a93caecf3237d90c9afd9bf48512ed698178097319b819c2ac599a1787408dR1-R170): Tests "3DS" auto-capture flows for both platform and connected merchant. - [`00008-NoThreeDSManualCapture.cy.js`](diffhunk://#diff-e881e4776f827fe169a5744fb20f7a1640183bc750ad304b3cdb2c4ec6627097R1-R270): Tests "No 3DS" manual capture flows, including payment confirmation and capture steps. **Enhancements to Global State Handling:** * In `00001-PlatformSetup.cy.js`, updated the global state to save and restore additional keys (`platformPublishableKey`, `platformApiKey`, and publishable keys for connected merchants) to facilitate the new test scenarios. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9ae4aff2012e41ef74f96280ddd67de6b150bd9d84ee53aedb8094c0f92d9781R29-R32) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9ae4aff2012e41ef74f96280ddd67de6b150bd9d84ee53aedb8094c0f92d9781R41-R49) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-9ae4aff2012e41ef74f96280ddd67de6b150bd9d84ee53aedb8094c0f92d9781R67) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-9ae4aff2012e41ef74f96280ddd67de6b150bd9d84ee53aedb8094c0f92d9781R103) **Test Data and Context Management Improvements:** * Each new test ensures correct setup and teardown of global state, switching context between platform and connected merchants as needed for accurate simulation of real-world payment flows. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e1393b2e4b3b130394efae31621155088dd26c189ca952cbd929bf6d9e382781R1-R186) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-15a93caecf3237d90c9afd9bf48512ed698178097319b819c2ac599a1787408dR1-R170) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-e881e4776f827fe169a5744fb20f7a1640183bc750ad304b3cdb2c4ec6627097R1-R270) These changes significantly expand our automated test coverage for platform-based payment scenarios, ensuring robust validation of both platform-mediated and direct merchant payment flows. ## How did you test it? ## Checklist - [ ] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [ ] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy` - [ ] I reviewed the submitted code - [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible

title: feat(business-profile): add payment_method_blocking configuration
body:
## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description Adds `payment_method_blocking` field to the business profile to allow merchants to configure card-level blocking rules. Merchants can block payments based on: - Card issuing country - Card type (credit/debit) - Card subtype - Card issuer ### Additional Changes - [x] This PR modifies the API contract - [x] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context Merchants need the ability to block specific categories of cards at the business profile level (e.g., block all prepaid cards, block cards issued in certain countries). This configuration is checked during payment processing via `validate_data_for_blocklist` and works alongside the existing fingerprint-based blocklist. ## How did you test it? - Verified compilation with `cargo check`
Business Profile - Create Request ``` curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/account/merchant_1773752499/business_profile' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'api-key: [REDACTED] --header 'Accept: */*' \ --data '{ "profile_name": "business", "return_url": "https://google.com/success", "enable_payment_response_hash": true, "redirect_to_merchant_with_http_post": false, "webhook_details": { "webhook_version": "1.0.1", "webhook_username": "ekart_retail", "webhook_password": "password_ekart@123", "webhook_url": "https://webhook.site", "payment_created_enabled": true, "payment_succeeded_enabled": true, "payment_failed_enabled": true }, "metadata": null, "intent_fulfillment_time": 900, "frm_routing_algorithm": null, "payout_routing_algorithm": null, "authentication_connector_details": null, "use_billing_as_payment_method_billing": true, "collect_shipping_details_from_wallet_connector": false, "collect_billing_details_from_wallet_connector": false, "is_connector_agnostic_mit_enabled": false, "payout_link_config": null, "outgoing_webhook_custom_http_headers": null, "payment_method_blocking": { "card": { "issuing_country": [ "IN", "US", "PK" ], "card_types": [ "Credit", "Debit" ], "card_subtypes": [ "Business", "Corporate" ], "issuers": [ "HDFC Bank", "ICICI Bank" ], "block_if_bin_info_unavailable": false } } }' ``` Response ``` { "merchant_id": "merchant_1773752499", "profile_id": "pro_swtFe3qaVzNh1ON7BMfv", "profile_name": "business", "return_url": "https://google.com/success", "enable_payment_response_hash": true, reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new `payment_method_blocking` configuration to business profiles, wiring it through the API/domain/diesel layers and exposing it in OpenAPI, while also extending the payments blocklist-guard hook to receive the business profile.

**Changes:**
- Adds a `payment_method_blocking` JSONB column to `business_profile` and updates Diesel schemas/models accordingly.
- Introduces `PaymentMethodBlockingConfig` / `CardBlockingConfig` types in `api_models` and `diesel_models`, plus bidirectional transformers.
- Threads `business_profile` into `guard_payment_against_blocklist` calls/signatures and updates OpenAPI specs to include the new schemas/fields.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 35 out of 35 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| migrations/2026-03-15-091519_add_payment_method_blocking_to_business_profile/up.sql | Adds `payment_method_blocking` JSONB column to `business_profile`. |
| migrations/2026-03-15-091519_add_payment_method_blocking_to_business_profile/down.sql | Drops `payment_method_blocking` column. |
| crates/router/src/types/transformers.rs | Adds API↔Diesel conversions for payment-method-blocking configs. |
| crates/router/src/types/api/admin.rs | Exposes field in profile responses; sets profile-create defaults (currently hard-codes `None`). |
| crates/router/src/db/events.rs | Updates test fixture profile creation to include new field. |
| crates/router/src/core/payments/operations/tax_calculation.rs | Updates blocklist guard hook signature to accept business profile. |
| crates/router/src/core/payments/operations/payments_incremental_authorization.rs | Updates blocklist guard hook signature to accept business profile. |
| crates/router/src/core/payments/operations/payments_extend_authorization.rs | Updates blocklist guard hook signature to accept business profile. |
| crates/router/src/

title: feat(payment-methods): Guest customer support in payments for modular pm
body:
## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [x] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description This pull request introduces significant changes to how `customer_id` is handled in the payment methods domain, primarily to support guest checkout tokenization. When it's a volatile PM, it's not going to DB, but as it goes to Redis, both Redis and DB share the common data model. So in v2, both the domain and the diesel model customer_id is an option, we need to ensure that pm without customer_id only goes to redis, not to db. For v1, only the domain payment method customer_id is an option; in Diesel, it is still a required field. v2 -> Domain::PaymentMethod and Diesel::PaymentMethod customer_id is option V1 -> Only Domain::PaymentMethod is an option. Other than guest customer support in payments for modular service, this pr solves 2 issues - Volatile pm getting updated in the payments call (added storage_type in v1 domain::PaymentMethod, its value is taken from create and retrieve response while transformation in payments call) - Payment method status is not getting updated post payment when customer_acceptance is passed with payment_token. [issue](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch-cloud/issues/15128#issuecomment-4109222274) Currently, volatile PMs are also being updated in the payments call, so based on the storage_type, we will decide to update the modular pm **Domain Model and Conversion Logic Updates:** - Made `customer_id` an `Option` in the `PaymentMethod` struct to allow for guest checkout scenarios, and updated all relevant conversions to require `customer_id` when interacting with storage or external systems. Added comments to clarify these requirements. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-897e1908cadfd041854264e488ef8f13483c5fe9c19c4689b9c29e9a72720603L61-R64) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-897e1908cadfd041854264e488ef8f13483c5fe9c19c4689b9c29e9a72720603R420-R423) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-897e1908cadfd041854264e488ef8f13483c5fe9c19c4689b9c29e9a72720603R566-R568) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-897e1908cadfd041854264e488ef8f13483c5fe9c19c4689b9c29e9a72720603R616-R618) **API and Controller Adjustments:** - Updated all controller and API response logic to handle `customer_id` as an `Option`, using `.get_required_value("customer_id")?` to enforce its presence where needed (e.g., database queries, locker operations, and responses). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827R952-R955) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1072-R1074) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827R1137-R1141) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1149-R1153) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1159-R1163) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1199-R1203) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1645-R1649) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827R1858-R1859) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1907-R1913) [[10]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1943-R1949) [[11]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1959-R1965) [[12]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L1969-R1975) [[13]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L2062-R2068) [[14]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L2119-R2125) [[15]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L4635-R4641) [[16]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L4788-R4794) [[17]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L4965-R4971) [[18]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L5326-R5332) **Migration and Helper Function Updates:** - Updated payment method migration and helper functions to handle the optionality of `customer_id`, ensuring `Some(customer_id)` is used when constructing domain objects. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-899c6e4040900d0c925c6d2fc8fb969528bd4538d8f35a2f4704d5452bf606c3L691-R691) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0cad9ec56cd7135aea31f7f8fb51ee0197f2125ebc87b716e9d023318361a827L169-R169) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c9c1119c4acac86f61c6eda88701efb49ba1f124a0dae12a9f2407065c02e275L281-R281) **Testing and Utility Changes:** - Modified tests and utility code to use `Some(id_type::CustomerId::default())` for `customer_id` to reflect the new optional type. **Code Cleanups:** - Minor import and trait adjustments to support the new code structure and remove unused code. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-897e1908cadfd041854264e488ef8f13483c5fe9c19c4689b9c29e9a72720603L13-R20) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-cc67c225e2abc3cf1508d5a810f5285e07e9a7c6ad306d5b480f45b32b0373d3L29) These changes collectively enable support for guest checkout tokenization while maintaining strict requirements for `customer_id` in all storage and external system interactions. ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [x] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context ## How did you test it?

commits.message: feat(payment-methods): update customer_id to support guest checkout and ensure proper handling in various modules
Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch into add-guest-customer-support-in-pm-mod
feat(payment-methods): make customer_id nullable for guest checkout support in v2 diesel
Merge branch 'add-guest-customer-support-in-pm-mod' of https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch into add-guest-customer-support-in-pm-mod
feat(payment_methods): add comments for better understanding
refactor(payments): simplify customer ID validation logic in payment methods
fix(payment_methods): improve error messages for missing customer_id
fix(payment_methods): remove comments about customer ID failure for guest checkout
fix(payment_methods): update error context for missing customer_id in payment method
feat(payment_methods): add storage_type field to v1 domain payment method structures and fix pm status bug
Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch into add-guest-customer-support-in-pm-mod
refactor(payment_methods): remove unused is_pm_volatile method and update related logic
fix(payment_confirm): update payment method ID assignment to use setter method

title: feat(core): add support for DecryptedWalletTokenDetailsForNetworkTransactionId PaymentMethod in UCS
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
- add support for DecryptedWalletTokenDetailsForNetworkTransactionId PaymentMethod in UCS
- Respective UCS PR - https://github.com/juspay/connector-service/pull/668

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

3. `loadtest/config`
-->


## Motivation and Context
<!--
Why is this change required? What problem does it solve?
If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here.

If you don't have an issue, we'd recommend starting with one first so the PR
can focus on the implementation (unless it is an obvious bug or documentation fix
that will have little conversation).
-->

title: feat(blocking): add profile-based payment method blocking with CardSubtype variants
body:
## Type of Change

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] New feature
- [x] Enhancement
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description

- Add comprehensive `CardSubtype` enum variants with proper `strum`/`serde` serialization for card subtype matching
- Implement `should_payment_be_blocked_by_profile_config` to block payments based on business profile's `payment_method_blocking` configuration (card network, card type, card subtype, issuing country)
- Pass `business_profile` through to `validate_data_for_blocklist` in the payment confirm flow

### Additional Changes

- [x] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

## Motivation and Context

Enable merchants to configure payment method blocking rules at the business profile level. This allows blocking payments based on card network, card type, card subtype, and issuing country without relying solely on the global blocklist.

## How did you test it?
<details>
<summary>1. Business Profile - Update</summary>

title: feat(connector): Add processing account id in payload MITs
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description This PR adds processing_id to the Payload MIT mandate request payload. Previously, MIT requests did not include the processing account identifier. This update extends PayloadMandateRequestData with an optional processing_id field and sets it while building the MIT request in transformers.rs. ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context ## How did you test it?
