title: refactor(euclid): add hybrid routing as a routing stage
body:
## Type of Change
<!-- Put an `x` in the boxes that apply -->

- [ ] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [x] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
This pull request refactors and simplifies the routing logic in the payments core, consolidating static and dynamic routing into a new "hybrid routing" approach. It removes the previous decision engine static routing logic, introduces new hybrid routing structures and flow, and updates function and type names to reflect these changes. The result is a more unified and maintainable routing pipeline that can flexibly support both static and dynamic routing features.

Key changes include:

**Hybrid Routing Refactor:**

- Replaces the previous static and dynamic routing split with a new hybrid routing approach, introducing `HybridRoutingStage`, `HybridRoutingInput`, and related logic to handle both static and dynamic routing in a unified way. (`crates/router/src/core/payments/routing.rs` [crates/router/src/core/payments/routing.rsL1420-R1535](diffhunk://#diff-f9f43be4eb6b360f7b6962459a4943e79db89c6c253da0c3ad2b5096ea78cd9bL1420-R1535))
- Removes the `DecisionEngineStaticRoutingStage` and its input struct, along with the associated static routing logic, in favor of the new hybrid routing flow. (`crates/router/src/core/payments/routing.rs` [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f9f43be4eb6b360f7b6962459a4943e79db89c6c253da0c3ad2b5096ea78cd9bL791-R792) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f9f43be4eb6b360f7b6962459a4943e79db89c6c253da0c3ad2b5096ea78cd9bL909-R863)

**API and Function Signature Updates:**

- Renames and updates routing functions: `perform_static_routing_with_de` is replaced by `perform_static_routing_locally`, and `perform_dynamic_routing_if_enabled` is replaced by `perform_hybrid_routing_if_enabled`, with updated parameters and usage throughout the codebase. (`crates/router/src/core/payments.rs` [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5d80996e506a98da00996ee40e9c9b453521759d44120257490bfba5915532c1L10776-R10776) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5d80996e506a98da00996ee40e9c9b453521759d44120257490bfba5915532c1L10785-L10797) `crates/router/src/core/payments/routing.rs` [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-f9f43be4eb6b360f7b6962459a4943e79db89c6c253da0c3ad2b5096ea78cd9bL1420-R1535)

**Hybrid Routing Data Structures:**

- Introduces new data structures for hybrid routing requests and responses, including `HybridRoutingRequest`, `HybridRoutingResponse`, and `HybridRoutingOutcome`, to facilitate the new routing flow. (`crates/router/src/core/payments/routing/utils.rs` [crates/router/src/core/payments/routing/utils.rsR70-R88](diffhunk://#diff-95124607f906a95147dce8d9c55918717d647d6373f4bd8aa3db4ffe7d573530R70-R88))

These changes streamline the routing process, making it easier to extend and maintain, and set the foundation for more advanced routing strategies in the future.

title: fix(users): Prevent TOTP secret from being cleared on status update
body: ## Type of Change - [x] Bugfix ## Description Fixed a bug where the TOTP secret was accidentally cleared when calling the `terminate_two_factor_auth` API. ### The Problem The `UserUpdate::TotpUpdate` variant used `Option` for `totp_secret` and `totp_recovery_codes` fields. When `terminate_two_factor_auth` API passed `None` for these fields (intending "don't update"), the conversion to `UserUpdateInternal` transformed `None` into `Some(None)`, which Diesel interpreted as "set to NULL", accidentally clearing the user's TOTP secret. ### The Fix Changed the field types to `Option>` to properly distinguish three states: - `None`: Don't update the field - `Some(None)`: Set field to NULL - `Some(Some(value))`: Update field to value ## Motivation and Context This fixes a critical bug where users lost their TOTP configuration when the 2FA termination flow updated only the status but accidentally cleared the secret. ## How to test it? - Sign up with a new user - Enable Two Factor Authentication - Download recovery codes - Sign out - Sign in again with TOTP Code ## Checklist - [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all` - [x] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy` - [x] I reviewed the submitted code - [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible
author: Grovy-3170

commits.message:
fix(diesel_models): prevent TOTP secret from being cleared on status update

The UserUpdate::TotpUpdate variant used Option<T> for totp_secret and
totp_recovery_codes fields. When terminate_two_factor_auth API passed
None for these fields (meaning "don't update"), the conversion to
UserUpdateInternal transformed None into Some(None), which Diesel
interpreted as "set to NULL", accidentally clearing the TOTP secret.

Changed the field types to Option<Option<T>> to properly distinguish:
- None: don't update the field
- Some(None): set field to NULL
- Some(Some(value)): update field to value

title: refactor(blocking): consolidate profile-based blocklist check into should_payment_be_blocked
body:
## Type of Change

- [ ] Bugfix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Enhancement
- [x] Refactoring
- [ ] Dependency updates
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] CI/CD

## Description

Consolidates the profile-based payment blocking check into the main blocklist check function. Previously, callers had to chain two separate blocking calls — one for fingerprint-based blocking and one for profile-config-based blocking. Now both checks happen inside a single function by passing the business profile as a parameter.

### Additional Changes

- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

## Motivation and Context

Reduces code duplication and ensures all callers of the blocklist check automatically get profile-level blocking without needing to explicitly handle the second check themselves.

## How did you test it?
<details>
<summary>Blocklist Check  Enabled</summary>

Request
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/blocklist/toggle?status=true' \
--header 'api-key: [REDACTED]
--header 'Accept: */*'
```

Response
```
{
    "blocklist_guard_status": "enabled"
}
```
</details>

```
--header 'x-tenant-id: public' \
--header 'Accept: */*' \
--data '{
    "key": "should_perform_eligibility_merchant_1774458655",
    "value": "true"
}
'
```

Response

```
}
```

commits.message:
refactor(blocking): consolidate profile-based blocklist check into should_payment_be_blocked

Pass `business_profile` into `should_payment_be_blocked` so it internally
calls `should_payment_be_blocked_by_profile_config`, removing the need for
callers to chain two separate blocking checks.

title: fix(connectors): [TSYS] change order of order_number in tsys auth request body
body: ## Type of Change - [x] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description This PR fixes the request body order of the TSYS contract for the auth request ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context fixes #9375 ## How did you test it? Unfortunately, I'm not able to test this changes against an actual sandbox as I've been in touch with TYSY and they only provide test credentials for certification purpose. Also [this](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/pull/5758) PR mention that there are not test credential for this project. I was able to reproduce mentioned in the issue by using the Sandbox inside the developer portal: That's because orderNumber is the wrong group as per API specification and should be placed in a different order. If we try to put (as in the PR) right after cvv2, the request is successful: 1. title: fix(payment_methods): resolve PSP tokenization issue in modular payment methods
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description Introduces the default_fallback_routing JSONB column to the business_profile table and implements dual-write across all v1 code paths that update default fallback routing configurations. This enables psp tokenization feature to perform a zero auth payment with mca that was created in v1. Make payment_method_subtype optional for the psp tokenization flow ### Additional Changes - [ ] This PR modifies the API contract - [ ] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context ## How did you test it? ### default fallback routing column being populated based on the mca operation #### Create merchant connector account -> stripe db record config table #### Create merchant connector account -> cybersource db record config table #### Update mca to disabled stripe db record config table #### update mca to enable stripe db record config table #### delete mca for cybersource db record config table #### update default fallback using the api ``` curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/routing/default/profile/pro_DYoNhXutdHCRFrIuQrub' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'api-key: dev_QaPSr47Y49ZW6gQYDzCgiFXrwR8AM8UDvyXgSH4tz2rrpAsKranBak8gG2p7DNRv' \ --data '[ { "connector": "cybersource", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_jyq4pZewaTOMUJPyhoMc" }, { "connector": "stripe", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_aJenlhrV9CiI36NfTNRa" } ]' ``` ``` { "profile_id": "pro_DYoNhXutdHCRFrIuQrub", "connectors": [ { "connector": "cybersource", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_jyq4pZewaTOMUJPyhoMc" }, { "connector": "stripe", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_aJenlhrV9CiI36NfTNRa" } ] } ``` config table db #### update default fallback using the api (re-order it again) ``` curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/routing/default/profile/pro_DYoNhXutdHCRFrIuQrub' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'api-key: dev_QaPSr47Y49ZW6gQYDzCgiFXrwR8AM8UDvyXgSH4tz2rrpAsKranBak8gG2p7DNRv' \ --data '[ { "connector": "stripe", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_aJenlhrV9CiI36NfTNRa" }, { "connector": "cybersource", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_jyq4pZewaTOMUJPyhoMc" } ]' ``` ``` { "profile_id": "pro_DYoNhXutdHCRFrIuQrub", "connectors": [ { "connector": "stripe", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_aJenlhrV9CiI36NfTNRa" }, { "connector": "cybersource", "merchant_connector_id": "mca_jyq4pZewaTOMUJPyhoMc" } ] } ``` config table db ### PSP tokenization #### create a v2 customer ``` curl --location 'http://localhost:3030/v1/customers' \ --header 'x-profile-id: pro_DYoNhXutdHCRFrIuQrub' \ --header 'Authorization: api-key=dev_QaPSr47Y49ZW6gQYDzCgiFXrwR8AM8UDvyXgSH4tz2rrpAsKranBak8gG2p7DNRv' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw '{ "name": "John Doe", "email": "guest@example.com" }' ``` ``` { "id": "12345_cus_019d24abc9837e30ab42ac9a8bd02468", "merchant_reference_id": null, "connector_customer_ids": null, "name": "John Doe", "email": "guest@example.com", "phone": null, "phone_country_code": null, "description": null, "default_billing_address": null, "default_shipping_address": null, "created_at": "2026-03-25T11:05:31.268Z", "metadata": null, "default_payment_method_id": null, "tax_registration_id": null, "document_details": null } ``` #### Create payment method session intent ``` curl --location 'http://localhost:3030/v1/payment-method-sessions' \ --header 'x-profile-id: pro_DYoNhXutdHCRFrIuQrub' \ --header 'Authorization: api-key=dev_QaPSr47Y49ZW6gQYDzCgiFXrwR8AM8UDvyXgSH4tz2rrpAsKranBak8gG2p7DNRv' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'api-key: api-key=dev_QaPSr47Y49ZW6gQYDzCgiFXrwR8AM8UDvyXgSH4tz2rrpAsKranBak8gG2p7DNRv' \ --data-raw '{ "customer_id": "12345_cus_019d24abc9837e30ab42ac9a8bd02468", "billing": { "address": { "first_name": "John", "last_name": "Dough" }, "email": "example@example.com" }, "storage_type": "persistent", "psp_tokenization": { "tokenization_type": "multi_use", "connector_id": "mca_aJenlhrV9CiI36NfTNRa" } }' ``` ``` { "id": "12345_pms_019d24ace4c578a0aadfcd0884a732c4", "customer_id": "12345_cus_019d24abc9837e30ab42ac9a8bd02468", "billing": { "address": { "city": null, "country": null, "line1": null, title: fix(payment-methods): fetch payment method details in payouts flow
body:
## Type of Change
- [x] Bugfix

## Description
Fixed payout method data not being fetched from card vault when using `payout_method_id` in payouts flow.

**Changes:**
- Added `force_fetch_card_from_vault` parameter to `get_pm_list_context` to enable card vault lookup for payouts
- Added `get_card_details_from_locker` method to PaymentMethodsController trait
- Set `force_fetch_card_from_vault=true` in payouts validator to ensure card details are retrieved from vault
- Fixed typo in error message for `expiry_year` field

### Additional Changes
- [ ] This PR modifies the API contract
- [ ] This PR modifies the database schema  
- [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables

## Motivation and Context
Fixes regression introduced in [PR #7786](https://github.com/juspay/hyperswitch/pull/7786) where card vault lookup was removed from `get_pm_list_context`. This broke recurring payouts functionality where `payout_method_id` should retrieve stored card details from vault.

## How did you test it?

<details>
    <summary>1. Create a recurring card payout</summary>

cURL

    curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/payouts/create' \
        --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        --header 'api-key: dev_ydIiVWyEGA60P3uQa2IHCKACVSRVHceIJEPrmSsb1TFfpJHfBmYRaMoqrrOiSab1' \
        --data '{"amount":100,"currency":"EUR","profile_id":"pro_pkpPuJzb8FHi5TYDj6ew","customer_id":"cus_UWxTZBStM3s2FzdDGGun","connector":["adyenplatform"],"description":"Its my first payout request","payout_type":"card","payout_method_data":{"card":{"card_number":"4111111111111111","expiry_month":"03","expiry_year":"2030"}},"billing":{"address":{"line1":"1467","line2":"Harrison Street","line3":"Harrison Street","city":"San Fransico","state":"CA","zip":"94122","country":"FR","first_name":"John","last_name":"Doe"}},"entity_type":"Individual","recurring":true,"metadata":{"ref":"123"},"confirm":true,"auto_fulfill":true}'

Response

    {"payout_id":"payout_s9nuyxhtsta0qQSKSaYC","merchant_id":"merchant_1752554602","merchant_order_reference_id":null,"amount":100,"currency":"EUR","connector":"adyenplatform","payout_type":"card","payout_method_data":{"card":{"card_issuer":null,"card_network":null,"card_type":null,"card_issuing_country":null,"bank_code":null,"last4":"1111","card_isin":"411111","card_extended_bin":null,"card_exp_month":"03","card_exp_year":"2030","card_holder_name":null}},"billing":{"address":{"city":"San Fransico","country":"FR","line1":"1467","line2":"Harrison Street","line3":"Harrison Street","zip":"94122","state":"CA","first_name":"John","last_name":"Doe"},"phone":null,"email":null},"auto_fulfill":true,"customer_id":"cus_UWxTZBStM3s2FzdDGGun","customer":{"id":"cus_UWxTZBStM3s2FzdDGGun","name":"John Nether","email":null,"phone":"6168205362","phone_country_code":"+1"},"client_secret":"payout_payout_s9nuyxhtsta0qQSKSaYC_secret_OxIBwSwo8HWWeZat8yYk","return_url":null,"business_country":null,"business_label":null,"description":"Its my first payout request","entity_type":"Individual","recurring":true,"metadata":{"ref":"123"},"merchant_connector_id":"mca_pjCOTcjEbqMrGm7rRLte","status":"initiated","error_message":null,"error_code":null,"profile_id":"pro_pkpPuJzb8FHi5TYDj6ew","created":"2025-07-15T04:44:24.793Z","connector_transaction_id":"38EBID67N4HZE5UD","priority":null,"payout_link":null,"email":null,"name":"John Nether","phone":"6168205362","phone_country_code":"+1","unified_code":null,"unified_message":null,"payout_method_id":"pm_G5g1xumF3AXWDPY8yl30"}

Note down payout method ID

</details>


<details>
    <summary>2. Create payout using payout_method_id</summary>

cURL

    curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/payouts/create' \
        --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        --header 'api-key: dev_ydIiVWyEGA60P3uQa2IHCKACVSRVHceIJEPrmSsb1TFfpJHfBmYRaMoqrrOiSab1' \
        --data-raw '{"payout_method_id":"pm_G5g1xumF3AXWDPY8yl30","amount":1,"currency":"EUR","customer_id":"cus_UWxTZBStM3s2FzdDGGun","email":"payout_customer@example.com","name":"John Doe","phone":"999999999","phone_country_code":"+65","description":"Its my first payout request","connector":["adyenplatform"],"billing":{"address":{"line1":"1467","line2":"Harrison Street","line3":"Harrison Street","city":"San Fransico","state":"CA","zip":"94122","country":"US","first_name":"John","last_name":"Doe"},"phone":{"number":"8056594427","country_code":"+91"}},"entity_type":"Individual","recurring":true,"metadata":{"ref":"123"},"confirm":true,"auto_fulfill":true}'

Response

    {"payout_id":"payout_QfSUC79LP4YDXjKwvn66","merchant_id":"merchant_1752554602","merchant_order_reference_id":null,"amount":1,"currency":"EUR","connector":"adyenplatform","payout_type":"card","payout_method_data":{"card":{"card_issuer":null,"card_network":null,"card_type":null,"card_issuing_country":null,"bank_code":null,"last4":"1111","card_isin":"411111","card_extended_bin":null,"card_exp_month":"03","card_exp_year":"2030","card_holder_name":null}},"billing":{"address":{"city":"San Fransico","country":"US","line1":"1467","line2":"Harrison Street","line3":"Harrison Street","zip":"94122","state":"CA","first_name":"John","last_name":"Doe"},"phone":{"number":"8056594427","country_code":"+91"},"email":null},"auto_fulfill":true,"customer_id":"cus_UWxTZBStM3s2FzdDGGun","customer":{"id":"cus_UWxTZBStM3s2FzdDGGun","name":"John Nether","email":null,"phone":"6168205362","phone_country_code":"+1"},"client_secret":"payout_payout_QfSUC79LP4YDXjKwvn66_secret_ArtMRkkTVFHDFsrTHMbE","return_url":null,"business_country":null,"business_label":null,"description":"Its my first payout request","entity_type":"Individual","recurring":true,"metadata":{"ref":"123"},"merchant_connector_id":"mca_pjCOTcjEbqMrGm7rRLte","status":"initiated","error_message":null,"error_code":null,"profile_id":"pro_pkpPuJzb8FHi5TYDj6ew","created":"2025-07-15T04:45:13.051Z","connector_transaction_id":"38EBHI67N4I9P28N","priority":null,"payout_link":null,"email":null,"name":"John Nether","phone":"6168205362","phone_country_code":"+1","unified_code":null,"unified_message":null,"payout_method_id":"pm_G5g1xumF3AXWDPY8yl30"}

</details>

## Checklist
- [x] I formatted the code `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] I addressed lints thrown by `cargo clippy`
- [x] I reviewed the submitted code
- [ ] I added unit tests for my changes where possible

I'm good with the changes, we need to have someone from core to check `payment_response.rs` file just to mitigate unknown issues. I understand that the status is changed only for FRM case but payment_response is currently used in every payment call so just need more eyes to verify.

title: feat(network_tokenization): Introduce NT credentials in domain tables
body: ## Type of Change - [ ] Bugfix - [x] New feature - [ ] Enhancement - [ ] Refactoring - [ ] Dependency updates - [ ] Documentation - [ ] CI/CD ## Description This pull request introduces support for storing and managing network tokenization credentials and data across merchant accounts, business profiles, and payment methods. The changes add new fields and data structures to accommodate network tokenization, specifically for integration with providers like Juspay. The updates span both the API models and the internal Diesel models to ensure end-to-end support for this new functionality. **Network Tokenization Credential Support** * Added the `NetworkTokeizationProviderCredentials` enum and `JuspayNetworkTokenizationCredentials` struct to represent provider-specific credentials, including API keys and encryption keys. * Updated API models (`MerchantAccountCreate`, `MerchantAccountUpdate`, `MerchantAccountResponse`, `ProfileCreate`, `ProfileUpdate`, `ProfileResponse`) to include an optional `network_tokenization_credentials` field. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c50d31f1c8ecb900891725f09240e0b31819cc74ddc380905bd8aafad949d30fR120-R123) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c50d31f1c8ecb900891725f09240e0b31819cc74ddc380905bd8aafad949d30fR400-R403) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c50d31f1c8ecb900891725f09240e0b31819cc74ddc380905bd8aafad949d30fR606-R609) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-c50d31f1c8ecb900891725f09240e0b31819cc74ddc380905bd8aafad949d30fR2319-R2322) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-c50d31f1c8ecb900891725f09240e0b31819cc74ddc380905bd8aafad949d30fR3076-R3079) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-c50d31f1c8ecb900891725f09240e0b31819cc74ddc380905bd8aafad949d30fR2696-R2699) **Database Model Changes** * Added an optional `network_tokenization_credentials` field (of type `Encryption`) to Diesel models for merchant accounts (`MerchantAccount`, `MerchantAccountSetter`, `MerchantAccountNew`, `MerchantAccountUpdateInternal`) and business profiles (`Profile`, `ProfileNew`, `ProfileUpdateInternal`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R58) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R94) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R133) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R258) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R362) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R396) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-4376e1d9c6b9ace8ea874059e9288c1a07f645567b9551ba892314115349ff05R436-R437) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-9580ab8a5056970f087fa2af0a8de98dae6bf038ade612e3c996b88b23ae6e11R87) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-9580ab8a5056970f087fa2af0a8de98dae6bf038ade612e3c996b88b23ae6e11R151) [[10]](diffhunk://#diff-9580ab8a5056970f087fa2af0a8de98dae6bf038ade612e3c996b88b23ae6e11R216) [[11]](diffhunk://#diff-9580ab8a5056970f087fa2af0a8de98dae6bf038ade612e3c996b88b23ae6e11R278) [[12]](diffhunk://#diff-9580ab8a5056970f087fa2af0a8de98dae6bf038ade612e3c996b88b23ae6e11R374-R375) **Payment Method Tokenization Data** * Introduced an optional `network_tokenization_data` field (of type `Encryption`) to payment method models (`PaymentMethod`, `PaymentMethodNew`, `PaymentMethodUpdate`, `PaymentMethodUpdateInternal`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR70) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR165) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deL264-R267) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR277) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR460) * Updated conversion and update logic for payment methods to handle the new `network_tokenization_data` field, ensuring proper propagation and fallback behavior. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR485) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR532-R533) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR565) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR589) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR610) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR636) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR661) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR685) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-3c313103a9ba3717e38b2381740e858458c390700aa94b382438e04aea2336deR701) ### Additional Changes - [x] This PR modifies the API contract - [x] This PR modifies the database schema - [ ] This PR modifies application configuration/environment variables ## Motivation and Context ## How did you test it? 1.
